symptom: Azure AD Connect versions 1.1.557.0 to 1.1.614.0 have issues with Change user sign-in task: (1) Password Hash Synchronization is automatically enabled when Pass-through Authentication is selected, (2) Incorrect prompt about disablement of Password Synchronization displayed when enabling PTA.
solution: Upgrade to Azure AD Connect version 1.1.644.0 or later. To mitigate: run Azure AD Connect, choose Customize synchronization options, uncheck Password synchronization on Optional features page. Optionally use Set-MsolUserPassword to set random passwords on impacted users (5 times each) to clear synced password hashes.

symptom: Azure AD Connect Health blade no longer showing up-to-date sync information. Health Insight Service crashes with Application Event 1000 for Microsoft.Identity.AadConnect.Health.AadSync.Host.exe (exception code 0xc0000374).
rootCause: Version mismatch between Azure AD Connect Synchronization Service and Azure AD Connect Health Agent for Sync. Occurs when either component was not successfully upgraded during last AAD Connect upgrade/auto-upgrade.
solution: Method 1: Manually upgrade Azure AD Connect to version 1.1.649.0 or later. Method 2: Manually reinstall Health Agent for Sync to a compatible version (3.0.127.0 or 3.0.129.0 depending on Sync Service version). After reinstall, register with Register-AzureADConnectHealthSyncAgent cmdlet.

symptom: Azure Seamless Single Sign-On not working on Windows 10 machines using MS Windows 10 Security baseline. IFrame shows Integrated Windows Authentication failed with status code 403 (Forbidden). Vanilla Windows 10 domain-joined machines work fine.
solution: Check Group Policies for Allowed Kerberos encryption type and set to Not Defined. For already affected machines: navigate to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\kerberos\parameters, delete the supportedencryptiontypes DWORD key, then reboot.

symptom: How to grant non-administrator accounts READ access to Event logs (Security, System, Application, DNS Server) on Domain Controllers and member servers (Server 2008 and above).

Support engineers delete AAD Connect Connector Space as a troubleshooting step for issues like phantom objects, join rules not working, transient objects, or objects not syncing. This is a risky operation that can cause data loss.
rootCause: Deleting Connector Space is a FIM/MIM procedure that should not be applied to AAD Connect. It can trigger massive export deletions if Sync Scheduler is still enabled, loses legitimate delete state, and prevents Product Group from investigating root causes.
solution: Run Full Import/Full Synchronization instead: Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Initial. If issue persists after exhausting all troubleshooting, raise an Escalation to AAD Connect Engineering team before attempting to delete the CS.

symptom: Object picker on child domain DC shows domains for which there is only a one-way incoming trust (no outgoing trust) when trying to add members to a child domain Domain Local Group. Root domain DCs are not affected.

Azure Active Directory fails to connect to SQL Database when using LocalDB. Error: SQL Server Network Interfaces: Unexpected error occurred inside a LocalDB instance API method call. Login timeout expired.
solution: Install SQL Express using a domain account to bypass using LocalDB.

symptom: After removing email address from an on-prem security group and running AAD Connect sync, the security group still shows MailEnabled=True with an initial domain (onmicrosoft.com) mail address in Azure AD.
rootCause: When an email-enabled security group is synced to AAD, an email address with the initial domain (onmicrosoft.com) is appended as secondary mail. Deleting the primary mail causes the initial domain address to become the new primary.
solution: Delete the group from Admin Portal and let it sync again, OR move the group to a non-syncing OU (filtered out) so the group is removed from cloud, then move it back.

symptom: How to assign users to Azure AD Dynamic Group based on their license details.

Steps to configure Azure Active Directory to emit group claims as group names (instead of SIDs/ObjectIDs) in SAML/OIDC tokens. Requires AAD Connect custom sync rules and optional claims configuration.

Sending the custom claims in a token to the applications which can be accomplished using the custom claims mapping. Custom claims mapping is a feature in Azure AD that allows us to extend the data sent in JWT tokens and we can use it to send information such as EmployeeID, department, mobile phone in the token. Steps: 1. Install Azure AD Preview PowerShell module 2. Create a new Azure AD policy with ClaimsMappingPolicy 3. Assign the policy to a SERVICE PRINCIPAL using Add-AzureADServicePrincipalPolicy. IMPORTANT: Turn on acceptMappedClaims to TRUE in the appManifest.

Linked sign-on scenarios: Customer has many instances of application with same identifier but different relay state values, and wanted those applications to be integrated with Azure AD using SAML based single sign on. Azure AD does not allow two instances of gallery apps active with two different RelayState configurations.
solution: Use Linked single sign-on (formerly Existing single sign-on). Create another instance of app with Linked single sign on configured. Copy the user access URL from the original SAML SSO app and append it with the desired RelayState value. Assign to different users/groups.

symptom: On-premises SharePoint uses wsfed federation protocol for integration with Azure AD. Wsfed uses SAML 1.1 (Token Issuance) but Azure AD by default is configured to issue SAML 2.0 token. This mismatch causes the SharePoint Application to go in infinite loop and unable to consume the token.
rootCause: Azure AD defaults to SAML 2.0 token issuance, but wsfed/SharePoint expects SAML 1.1.
solution: Configure Azure AD to issue SAML 1.1 token for the service principal using PowerShell: Import Initialize.ps1, run Add-TokenIssuancePolicy with -SamlTokenVersion 1.1, then Set-PolicyToServicePrincipal.

symptom: Guest Users created manually in Azure AD using PowerShell (New-MsolUser with -UserType Guest) are unable to login to SharePoint site. After changing one-time password, user gets error: Something went wrong. The #EXT# string in the UPN causes the failure.
rootCause: The Usertype needs to be Member in AAD or the #EXT# string in the user name is causing the failure.
solution: Option 1: Change the member type to Member using Set-MsolUser -UserPrincipalName UPN -UserType Member. Option 2: Replace #EXT# in username with -ext.

symptom: Unable to configure Alternate Login ID using description attribute for ADFS. Customer needed an attribute other than mail as some users had UPN over 12 characters and relying applications had a 12 character limit.
rootCause: The Description Attribute is not replicated in Global Catalog PAS (Partial Attribute Set).
solution: Include description attribute to be part of PAS on the Global Catalog and allow it to be indexed for searches. Once replication happened between GCs, the command to set Alternate Login ID with Description Attribute worked.

symptom: AAD Connect AutoUpgrade stuck in Suspended state. PKS Agent acts as if Disabled (never checks for new releases). Registry key UpdateCheckEnabled is set to False (0) when it should be True (1). Affects ~80K tenants.
rootCause: Bug introduced in build 1.1.524.0 (May 2017): UpdateCheckEnabled registry key is set to False whenever AutoUpgrade state is set to Suspended. Fixed in build 1.1.750.0.
solution: Manual upgrade to build 1.1.750.0 or later. Alternatively, run the Repair-ADSyncDiagsAutoUpgradeState PowerShell script which fixes the registry key and restarts AzureADConnectHealthSyncMonitor service.

symptom: VSTS will no longer allow new MSA users with custom domain names. Azure AD tenant needs to be connected to VSTS. When trying to disconnect AAD tenant from VSTS, AAD tenant disconnection failed error occurs.
solution: Connect Azure AD to Team Services account via VSTS settings. Clear cached credentials at %userprofile%/local settings/application data/Microsoft/VisualStudio Services/7.0, then re-authenticate.

symptom: Azure new PowerShell Get-AzureADDevice does not have a deviceID operator built in. It only has Get-AzureADDevice -ObjectId, making it impossible to look up devices by deviceID directly.

Unable to access the Relying Party configured on ADFS. Error: POLICY0018: Query to attribute store Active Directory failed: The supplied credential is invalid. Error code: 49, LdapErr: DSID-0C09056D, AcceptSecurityContext error, data 569. Network trace shows BindResponse: Status: Invalid Credentials from DC.
rootCause: Missing Registry Value MSOIDSSP under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\Security Packages.
solution: Add MSOIDSSP value under registry HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\Security Packages and reboot the server.

symptom: Multi-valued extension attributes (msExchExtensionCustomAttribute1-5) synchronized to Azure AD through AADConnect appear to be successfully populated on Export, but either do not actually get populated in Azure AD or get populated but no data access API exposes these attributes.
Unable to change UPN from user@contoso.com to user@contoso.onmicrosoft.com. Error: Set-MsolUserPrincipalName: Access Denied. You do not have permissions to call this cmdlet.
rootCause: There is an existing stale object entry in Azure AD which has to be removed at ORGID side.
solution: Run GME ID Consistency checker and submit ICM to Orgid team to remove the stale object entry.

symptom: When attempting to delete an Autopilot Deployment Profile, error: Cannot delete <AutopilotProfile Name>. The profile is assigned to groups. You must unassign all groups from this profile before you can delete it.
rootCause: This occurs when: 1) An active group is still assigned to the Autopilot Deployment Profile, or 2) A group was assigned to the profile but deleted from Azure AD before removing the assignment, leaving an orphaned link that cannot be removed via portal.
solution: Remove all groups from Intune portal (Device Enrollment > Windows enrollment > Autopilot deployment profiles > Assignment). If the group was deleted from AAD, use Graph Explorer DELETE request to /beta/deviceManagement/windowsAutopilotDeploymentProfiles/<ProfileID>/assignments/<ProfileID>_<GroupID> to remove the orphaned assignment, then delete the profile.

symptom: How to publish on-prem SAML Claim Aware application using Azure AD Application Proxy. Covers configuring SAML application federated with Azure AD using AAD-AP, including pre-authentication setup and SSO configuration.

AAD Connect Synchronization Service Manager delta or full AD import operations fail with "No DirSync control data returned." Connection Log shows "Failed-Search" status. Event ID 6050 (ADSync) reports connectivity issues on the management agent.
rootCause: TCP packet fragmentation causes data integrity issues. Improper or non-matching TCP Offloading settings between Domain Controller and AAD Connect server cause PayLoadLen mismatches during packet reassembly. Outdated networking drivers may also contribute.
solution: On both Domain Controller and AAD Connect server: disable all TCP Offloading options in advanced network adapter settings, enable Receive Side Scaling (RSS), update networking adapter drivers, then reboot both servers and run a delta import.

symptom: After migrating users with ADMT and SID history from source to target domain, users cannot access file shares in the source domain. Effective permissions for the migrated user show empty on the file server, despite SID filtering being disabled between forests.
rootCause: Expected behavior of Built-in groups. The "Domain Users" built-in group membership from the source domain does not carry over via SID history for file share permission evaluation.
solution: Option 1: Create a custom group in the source domain, add the migrated user, and grant that group permissions on the share. Option 2: Add the target domain "Domain Users" group directly to the folder permissions.

symptom: SQLVDI and SQLWriter errors on servers running Azure AD Connect cause VM backup failures. VSS error: SqlServerWriter state VSS_WS_FAILED_AT_PREPARE_SNAPSHOT (0x800423f4). LocalDB errorlog shows BackupVirtualDeviceSet::Initialize CoCreateInstance failure with 0x800703fa (Illegal operation attempted on a registry key marked for deletion).
rootCause: AAD Connect uses LocalDB under AAD_xxx user context and forces user instance timeout to 65535 (preventing LocalDB shutdown). When the sync session ends, Windows unloads the user registry but LocalDB stays active. Subsequent VDI/COM calls fail because COM classes were unloaded with the orphaned registry.
solution: Workaround 1: Activate Local Policy "Do not forcefully unload the user registry at user logoff" (KB2287297, may need reboot). Workaround 2: Revert LocalDB user instance timeout to default (60 min idle shutdown). Check AAD Connect product defect #315878 for permanent fix status.

symptom: SSPR_0029 error on Password Reset Self Service Portal: "We are unable to reset your password due to an error in your on-premises configuration." Event 33004 PasswordResetService: "The password could not be updated because the management agent credentials were access denied." Event 6329 ADSync: unexpected error during password set operation.
rootCause: AAD Connect Password Writeback uses SAM RPC. A Group Policy SAM RPC hardening setting (Network access: Restrict clients allowed to make remote calls to SAM) on the DC restricts SAM connections to Admins only, and the AAD Connect service account is not a domain admin.
solution: Add the AAD Connect AD DS connector account to the allowed SAM RPC callers list. Use PowerShell: Import-Module "C:Program FilesMicrosoft Azure Active Directory ConnectAdSyncConfigAdSyncConfig.psm1"; Get-ADSyncADConnectorAccount to identify the account, then add it to the GPO security descriptor.

symptom: Customers see the following prompt on the top right corner when they are in Azure Portal UI within the Enterprise Applications.Clicking the above prompt gives them the following page.
rootCause: As the second page above indicates, this prompt happens if there is a Reply URL that starts with https://127.0.0.1:444/...This was a Reply URL that was added by MS earlier and is known to cause signin issues. Hence MS PG decided to remove it. However being on the customer's tenant, we did not want to remove it from the backend.This configuration can be verified by using ASC ( Azure Support Center) as well.
solution: 1. If the customer hasn't added the Reply URL above using the Application Registrations node, then they can do one of the following:a. In the second dialog above, there is a button at the bottom on the page to "Remove Incorrect Reply URL". Simply Clicking it will fix it.b. They can remove it manually from the Application Registrations node, ApplicationName. Properties and Reply URLs. Simply highlight the one with the above Ip 127.0.0.1 and delete it.c. Lastly they can do within the Manifest for the application in the Application Registrations. NOTE: Editing the manifest can make the application unstable and hence use proper caution to just remove the one line under Reply URL section.2. If the customer added the URL themselves manually and are experiencing signin issues, then please test removing the reply URL and confirm if the issues go away. If the customer wants to keep the URL, then please initiate a discussion in appropriate Teams channel or  open ICM for PG for comments

administrator fills the required fields for provisioning and after click on Test Connection the following error occurs:

You appear to have entered invalid credentials. Pleas confirm you are
using the correct information for an administrative account.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Troubleshooting steps:


 Required Information


Following
information is required for further troubleshooting


 Tenant Name (initial domain)
 Time and Date when the issue
     occurred
 Time Zone of the machine,
     where this config was made


 

 


 Open ASC and convert the
     Initial Domain Name to tenant ID


It
is a good thing to ask the customer for the tenant ID, where the issue
occurred, because our support request might be related to another tenant of the
same customer.

  

 


 Open Kusto Explorer and run
     the following query


 

IfxUsageEvent

|
where contextId
== "06770ba4-zzzz-xxxx-yyyy-929feb7dcf78"
// this is the tenantID

|
where env_time
> ago(4d) 

|
where message
contains "validateCredentials"

|
project env_time,
correlationId, message

 

Returned
a list of recent attempts to validate credentials via the portal. 

 

 


 From the output of 4 took the
     correlationId from newest attempt, which matched your screenshot�s
     timestamp, and ran this:


 

IfxUsageEventWithCorrIdUrl

|
where correlationId
== "837cb19e-xxxx-yyyy-zzzz-86ebb31e54f2"
| project url

 

And
from that it gave me a Jarvis URL 

 

 


 Copy the url and open in in a
     browser


 and remov the default filter of Warning events

and
sorte by env_seqNum � timestamps in these logs only go down to the seconds, 

so
we�ll have a whole bunch of events at say e.g. 03-21-2019 14:22:20 and the
ordering is incorrect. 

The
env_seqNum attribute is the actual �what order did these events occur in�
counter and sorting by that makes them 100% accurate in order. 

Once
I sorted by env_seqNum, scrolling down to the end I was able to find three
things � a request out to ServiceNow, a response from ServiceNow, and an error
generated by the response:

 



  

 

 


 In this output we see
     following important messages:


 

%%pii_WEB REQUEST :: Performing an

<getRecords xmlns="http://www.service-now.com/sys_user">

And
then they respond with: 

 

%%pii_WEB RESPONSE :: Received

<getRecordsResponse></getRecordsResponse> Request: WEB REQUEST
:: Performing an operation on a Web resource. Resource: https://vi*****.service-now.com/sys_user.do?SOAP Operation: http://www.service-now.com/sys_user/getRecords Headers: Content
Type: Data: <getRecords xmlns="http://www.service-now.com/sys_user">
<user_name>azure.provisioning</user_name> </getRecords>

 

All
of the stuff in the response after the highlighted yellow part is a repeat of
our request � ie: they say �here is your response, also after my response here
is your original request so you know what
I am responding to�.

 

Our request out to ServiceNow was �give me the user
azure.provisioning�s information�, and they respond with <getRecordsResponse></getRecordsResponse>, ie: a completely empty response. No error, but no
data.

 

Looking
one line down from that, we see:

 

Error code:
ServiceNowAdministratorImport Scope: RunProfile Transient? False Tenant
Actionable? True Body:
Microsoft.ActiveDirectory.Connector.UserReportableException: Although we received no

should really be surfacing that error to the customer but it looks like we

When Conditional Access Policy is created to force MFA
on Exchange Online (EXO) and/or SharePoint Online (SPO) cloud application, and
the users open the browser and try to create Word/Excel/PowerPoint from https://portal.office.com portal. The
configured Conditional Access Policy will prevent them with the following
error:
rootCause:
When user tries to create Word/Excel/PowerPoint document from https://portal.office.com portal, the selected application tries to access Exchange Online (EXO) and/or SharePoint Online (SPO) using non-interactive login. And since MFA does not complete with non-interactive login, the authentication will fail, and the user will get the above error. From Fiddler trace: GET: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/authorize?client_id=00000002-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000&redirect_uri=https%3a%2f%2foutlook.office365.com%2fowa%2f&resource=00000002-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000&response_mode=form_post&response_type=code+id_token&scope=openid&msafed=0&client-request-id=ed2367c1-edcc-4637-9a07-cd99de2deffe&protectedtoken=true&prompt=none&login_hint=user1%40zmaililab.onmicrosoft.com&nonce=636890311591198484.cf66b484-c576-4825-ba79-cf6f593e7cbf&state=pYvBisIwFEXb8VvirrFp2rRZFBHEpQwjfkAaXmigMSVJrc4Pz2_43LkfePdxuNyTZ1m2wXxh8hJf1gouOllyxhrJmOzqrqbaCDEgFLppRVF3VVMMqpUF9qaRHFo9mBzdv3znV7W7LDbBBcLdavgO_vGkKs6PffyorxHCWTnoFwRG6vLXKTvZSQ3U35zVwUdvEtXebT-1H0hLuF3D1I8pzZHwA6lOeOu6Um8MLt7Ku-MntaSR8GNFKjF6B4hsG_7pq9neIfTsBQ Response: Working...
From logs Miner: OAuth2 authorization request: { %%pii_removed%%}; client_id=00000002-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000&redirect_uri=https%3a%2f%2foutlook.office365.com%2fowa%2f&resource=00000002-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000&response_mode=form_post&response_type=code+id_token&scope=openid&msafed=0&client-request-id=ed2367c1-edcc-4637-9a07-cd99de2deffe&protectedtoken=true&prompt=none&xxxxxx=%7bEmailHidden%7d&nonce=636890311591198484.cf66b484-c576-4825-ba79-cf6f593e7cbf&xxxxxx=pYvBisIwFEXb8VvirrFp2rRZFBHEpQwjfkAaXmigMSVJrc4Pz2_43LkfePdxuNyTZ1m2wXxh8hJf1gouOllyxhrJmOzqrqbaCDEgFLppRVF3VVMMqpUF9qaRHFo9mBzdv3znV7W7LDbBBcLdavgO_vGkKs6PffyorxHCWTnoFwRG6vLXKTvZSQ3U35zVwUdvEtXebT-1H0hLuF3D1I8pzZHwA6lOeOu6Um8MLt7Ku-MntaSR8GNFKjF6B4hsG_7pq9neIfTsBQ&cxh_inclusive=False OAuth2 error response: https://outlook.office365.com/owa/ -- (error: interaction_required, error_description: AADSTS50076: Due to a configuration change made by your administrator, or because you moved to a new location, you must use multi-factor authentication to access '00000002-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000'. Trace ID: 79e01448-7e78-470a-aa3b-673018da1700 Correlation ID: ed2367c1-edcc-4637-9a07-cd99de2deffe Timestamp: 2019-03-24 13:32:44Z, state: pYvBisIwFEXb8VvirrFp2rRZFBHEpQwjfkAaXmigMSVJrc4Pz2_43LkfePdxuNyTZ1m2wXxh8hJf1gouOllyxhrJmOzqrqbaCDEgFLppRVF3VVMMqpUF9qaRHFo9mBzdv3znV7W7LDbBBcLdavgO_vGkKs6PffyorxHCWTnoFwRG6vLXKTvZSQ3U35zVwUdvEtXebT-1H0hLuF3D1I8pzZHwA6lOeOu6Um8MLt7Ku-MntaSR8GNFKjF6B4hsG_7pq9neIfTsBQ, )
solution:
Since this is non-interactive login, MFA
will not trigger, and the MFA popup will not show to the end user. Hence, creating
Word/Excel/PowerPoint documents from office portal will fail.

Therefore, we need to modify the configured
Conditional Access Policy to target �All Cloud Applications� instead of targeting
Exchange Online (EXO) and/or SharePoint Online (SPO).

The idea behind that, once the user open
office poral, he will be forced to provide MFA. Then when he tries to create Word/Excel/PowerPoint
documents from Office portal, MFA will be required again, but in this scenario,
MFA claim is satisfied in the browser session cookies from the first MFA at the
begging, and the user will be able to create the needed documents successfully.

 

Note: we are targeting �All
Cloud Applications� because there is no cloud application to target office
portal yet.

This article is still in development and is not to be shared with customers yet.
  
  
     
  
  
    Applies to customers who have enabled Conditional Access policies for iOS and macOS device platforms.
  
  
     
  
  
    For any questions on this issue, please contact Sandeep Deo 
    
      sandeo@microsoft.com
    
    .
  
  
     
  
  
    We recently discovered that Apples launch of iOS13 scheduled for mid-September will introduce a breaking change that could impact Microsoft Azure AD and Intune customers that use Conditional Access policies in their organization. This notice is intended to help you understand the breaking change from Apple and evaluate the impact to your organization, and to provide several recommendations from Microsoft.
  
  
     
  
  
    What is the breaking change?
  
  
    In iOS13, Apple is introducing a new OS, the iPadOS. All iPads that update to iOS 13+ will have their OS updated from iOS to iPadOS. While some of this new OS will behave similarly to iOS, there are key apps, such as Safari, that are identified as and operate now as macOS. Since CA policies are often applied on an OS- or App-specific basis, this change could affect your security and compliance of any iPad device that upgrades to iPadOS.
  
  
     
  
  
    What is impacted?
  
  
    Theres a set of apps used for Conditional Access that will be affected since these apps are now macOS-identified apps. In reviewing your CA policies, youll want to focus on whether you provide access through any compliance or security checks using the affected app categories. 
  
  
     
  
  
    Below are the app categories that will 
    impact 
    enforcement of your CA policies on iPad running iPadOS.
  
  
    
    Safari browser
  
  
    
    Apple Native mail
  
  
    
    Apps that 
    do not use
     Microsoft identity platform 
    
      v2.0 authentication libraries
    
     or 
    
      v1.0 authentication libraries
    
  
  
     
  
  
    There is 
    no impact
     to the below app categories.
  
  
    
    All Microsoft apps including Edge browser
  
  
    
    Chrome browser
  
  
    
    
      Apps
    
     that use 
    
      Intune App SDK
    
     or 
    
      App wrapping tool for iOS
    
  
  
    
    Apps that use Microsoft identity platform 
    
      v2.0 authentication libraries
    
     or 
    
      v1.0 authentication libraries
    
  
  
     
  
  
    Lets walk through a couple of scenarios that could be impacted before we get to Microsofts recommendations. 
  
  
    1)
    Youve setup a CA policy that requires an approved client app for email access and you have no policy configured for macOS. In this case, once a device updates to iPadOS, the 3
    
      rd
    
     party mail app will not enforce the CA policy. 
  
  
    2)
    Youve setup a CA policy that requires a compliant device in order to access company resources from an iPad. However, you did not configure a macOS compliance policy, as your executives have macs and requested exemption from all CA policies. Now, when the iPads update to iOS 13, users will be able to access corporate resources from iPads on a non-compliant device since CA is not targeted to macOS devices. 
  
  
    3)
    3  (we can put in a third example, but for now, lets see what happens at the LT level). 
  
  
     
  
  
    What is Microsofts recommendation?
  
  
    The following table illustrates the impact in detail and the recommendation from Microsoft with respect to a CA policy configuration you may have in place for your organization.
  
  
     
  
  
    
      
        
          
            Conditional Access policy configuration
          
        
        
          
            Impact
          
        
        
          
            Recommendation
          
        
      
      
        
          
            iOS
          
        
        
          
            macOS
          
        
      
      
        
          
            You have one of these configured: 
          
          
            1)
            Require approve client app/Require app protection policy
          
          
            2)
            Require approve client app/Require app protection policy
          
          
            AND
          
          
            Require compliant device
          
          
            3)
            Require approve client app/Require app protection policy
          
          
            OR
          
          
            Require compliant device
          
          
            4)
            Require compliant device
          
          
             
          
        
        
          
            No policy
          
        
        
          
            Accessing your organizations resources from apps in the impacted app category described above will result in bypassing approved client app /app protection policy/ compliant device policy and providing unfettered access.
          
        
        
          
            
            We highly recommend configuring a Conditional Access policy for macOS to require a compliant device.
          
          
            
            If this is not an option for you at this time, we recommend falling back to using Require MFA. 
          
          
            Note
            : Having no policy for macOS will provide zero security.
          
        
      
      
        
          
            Require compliant device
          
        
        
          
            Accessing your organizations resources from apps in the impacted app category described above will result in bypassing approved client app /app protection policy and asking user to enroll their device with Microsoft Intune.
          
          
            Note:
             No impact if you have #3 or #4 configured for iOS. 
          
        
        
          
            Educate users to use apps from the non-impacted app category.
          
        
      
      
        
          
            Require MFA
          
        
        
          
            Accessing your organizations resources from apps in the impacted app category described above will result in bypassing approved client app /app protection policy/ compliant device policy and prompting user for MFA.
          
        
        
          
            
            If this behavior will not work for your organization, we recommend that you configure a Conditional Access policy for macOS to require a compliant device. 
          
          
            
            If this is not an option for you at this time, we recommend you educate users to use apps from the non-impacted app category described above.
          
        
      
      
        
          
            Block Access
          
        
        
          
            Accessing your organizations resources from apps in the impacted app category described above will result in bypassing approved client app /app protection policy and blocking access.
          
        
        
          
            If this behavior will not work for your organization, we recommend that you educate users to use apps from the non-impacted app category.
          
        
      
      
        
          
            Terms of Use (consent per device)
          
        
        
          
            No Terms of Use (consent per device)
          
        
        
          
            Accessing your organizations resources from apps in the impacted app category described above will result in not prompting users for consenting to terms of use.
          
        
        
          
            Educate users to use apps from the non-impacted app category.
          
        
      
    
  
  
     
  
  
    
      
     
  
  
    Will there be additional

rootCause: The on-premises ActiveDirectory attribute �Manager� is a reference attribute and contains the distinguished name of the user who is the user's manager. As a workaround to bypass this scenario, you can try to

are two possible scenarios:Option 1: Assign using a contact objectYou can create in forest A that will correspond to the

What is this attribute and how is it used? The process that drives populating and using
this attribute is part of the Next Generation Credentials Technology (commonly
known as � NGC). More
about NGC and Microsoft passport: TechCommunity: Microsoft Passport and Azure AD: Eliminating passwords one device at a time!   There are multiple services and devices that are
capable of NGC. Windows hello for business is just one of our services that
uses the password less authentication flow. For your reference, in
the following article you have an example attached on how the Windows hello for
business flow works and how the value for msDS-KeyCredentialLink is
processed. Learn: Hybrid Azure AD joined provisioning in a key trust deployment in a managed environment   The msDS-KeyCredentialLink user
attribute in Active Directory stores the asymmetric key (public part) needed
for the user account identity in the authentication flow. The key stored under
the msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute will be used to first identify the ser
on-premise and then authenticate him. In case of Windows Hello for Business the
asymmetric key written under msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute needs to be
available both on the user and machine object, as it is directly related to
both.  The searchableDeviceKey attribute associated to the user object Azure
Active Directory is populated once the user has a linked device that can
perform NGC (there are numerous types of devices & applications that can
use NGC and you can have linked to your  Azure Active Directory account, for
e.g. authenticator app, USB drive for fingerprint scan, biometrics scan etc.). How is this attribute populated and synchronized?Once a device is linked to the Azure AD user
and searchableDeviceKey calculation is completed, the following flow is being
followed by the Azure Active Directory Connect tool to writeback msDS-KeyCredentialLink.
The value is first being read from Azure AD nd provisioned to the AADC
metaverse by the following rule: New-ADSyncRule  ` -Name 'In from AAD - User NGCKey' `-Identifier '53d9ba9f-f519-443f-ad24-b5344a757XXX' `-Description '' `-Direction 'Inbound' `-Precedence X `-PrecedenceAfter '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' `-PrecedenceBefore '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' `-SourceObjectType 'user' `-TargetObjectType 'person' `-Connector 'b891884f-051e-4a83-95af-2544101c9XXX' `-LinkType 'Join' `-SoftDeleteExpiryInterval 0 `-ImmutableTag 'Microsoft.InfromAADUserNGCKey.001' `-OutVariable syncRule Add-ADSyncAttributeFlowMapping  `-SynchronizationRule $syncRule[0] `-Destination 'deviceKey' `-FlowType 'Expression' `-ValueMergeType 'Update' `-Expression 'IIF(IsNull([searchableDeviceKey]), NULL, IIF(CNum(Mid([searchableDeviceKey],1,1))=0, Mid([searchableDeviceKey],1,7+CNum(Mid([searchableDeviceKey],5,2))) & CBin(Len(Thumbprint(Mid([searchableDeviceKey],8+CNum(Mid([searchableDeviceKey],5,2)),Len([searchableDeviceKey])))),2) & CBin(2,1) & Thumbprint(Mid([searchableDeviceKey],8+CNum(Mid([searchableDeviceKey],5,2)),Len([searchableDeviceKey]))) & Mid([searchableDeviceKey],8+CNum(Mid([searchableDeviceKey],5,2)),Len([searchableDeviceKey])), NULL))' `-OutVariable syncRule In short, the algorithm from the ADSyncAttributeFlowMappingExpression evaluates the searchableDeviceKey attribute from the user
present in Azure Active Directory and checks if the value is compliant. If this
is met, we will update the deviceKey attribute (present in the
Metaverse) with the value of the searchableDeviceKey attribute from
Azure Active Directory.    SearchableDeviceKey
attribute value present in MSODS:   {<###iceKey ###ge="#" ###Identifier="#/###IVKGN6dfEighnuKrHv+###aBti4MtpGwnO8GFQ=" ###ationTime="###9-##-###19:##:##.###4674Z" ###iceId="###69004-###a-###4-###c-###ccc85d700" ###tomKeyInformation="###AAQACAAAAAAAAAAAA" ###ns="###p://###emas.###rosoft.###/###ine/###ectoryservices/###nge/###8/##"><###Material>###BMQAIAAADAAAAAAEAAAAAAAAAAAAAAQAB4blIn9hQoiBrx5GjTrUrlZhf99TcOnydK9Ttmf5crA4ab3MLEunVcE69ubI2OYjZMU9btdRPGfgB4xbqvWZlWxxNFv6PgRo7ZlS8rEa2Draef8t4VMxR7Dw/###C6YFl1htjrZo0qOOuaAEj3g2QpFmF/###C4eExvYnbXgDt52QgXqB6kEIY7gJHHVGrFDgHWQsfZzDuBPuelEp377exMt5KZBcocz6gmyxnOha10hOEynQFlZO9idv2vKiIhUcd4QqsRXR8vtoGyd5ANHTaK2H+###JmmA4I0CxTiOD8qnfGXFuoVlcojv7O+###GF4MEQGiHkxmJSd5l22t1QEQ==</###Material></###iceKey>}  Next in the synchronization flow, the deviceKey
attribute will be exported from the Metaverse to the local Active Directory
connector space, processed by the following rule:   New-ADSyncRule  `-Name 'Out to AD - User NGCKey' `-Identifier '515d25ba-4ae7-4622-b67a-6f3811f8eXXX' `-Description '' `-Direction 'Outbound' `-Precedence X `-PrecedenceAfter '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' `-PrecedenceBefore '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' `-SourceObjectType 'person' `-TargetObjectType 'user' `-Connector '58b27e0e-e269-488a-8cdf-32899a06cXXX' `-LinkType 'Join' `-SoftDeleteExpiryInterval 0 `-ImmutableTag 'Microsoft.OuttoADUserNGCKey.001' `-OutVariable syncRuleAdd-ADSyncAttributeFlowMapping  `-SynchronizationRule $syncRule[0] `-Source @('deviceKey') `-Destination 'msDS-KeyCredentialLink' `-FlowType 'Direct' `-ValueMergeType 'Update' `-OutVariable syncRuleNew-Object  `-TypeName 'Microsoft.IdentityManagement.PowerShell.ObjectModel.ScopeCondition' `-ArgumentList 'sourceAnchor','','ISNOTNULL' `
               -OutVariable condition0     This basically translates in: If the scoping
condition for this rule is met (user is synchronized so sourceAnchor is
populated), the value of the deviceKey will be updated to the msDS-KeyCredentialLink
attribute. This attribute is further-on exported to the user object present in
Active Directory, if the Active Directory Connector account has the right
permissions on ADDS.

symptom: When customers review their sign in Logs, they notice that the User IDs show up as User: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000Username: domainName.comUser ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000Alternate sign-in name: Application: ApplicationXYZApplication ID: Resource: ResourceNameXYZResource ID:  Client app:  The above kind of logs can have both success and failure as their Status.This in-turn affects their log analysis logic, where they try to find out malicious sign ins, by filtering out the sign in logs with UPNs that contain domain names other than the ones that are already present as verified in their domain, or the domain names that they have guest users from. This also raises concern to the customers, since they do not find who the user is, and they suspect malicious activity in their tenants.
rootCause: This happens due to setup of Tenant restrictions.Tenant restrictions on a network, is basically setup using a proxy device that inserts headers into traffic to Azure AD endpoints. The two headers that are inserted are  :Restrict-Access-To-Tenants:  (use a value of <permitted tenant list>, which is a comma-separated list of tenants you want to allow users to access.)Restrict-Access-Context: (use a value of a single directory ID, declaring which tenant is setting the tenant restrictions.)For more on how this is setup, please refer : https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/manage-apps/tenant-restrictions#configurationLet us consider an infrastructure of Tenant A, where they have configured their internet proxy device in a way that inserts headers in their network, as follows :Restrict-Access-To-Tenants: TenantA.onmicrosoft.com, TenantB.onmicrosoft.com, TenantC.onmicrosoft.comRestrict-Access-Context: DirectoryIDofTenantALet us consider the following scenarios now :1) User from tenant A, accesses an application from tenant A internally (making use of Tenant A's Infrastructure's network, where the headers are inserted by the proxy devices that exist in the infrastructure, as per the tenant restrictions feature)  In this scenario, a Sign-In log will be generated with all the details, in two places :               -- Sign in Logs of Tenant A               -- Azure Active Directory blade > Overview > Other Capabilities > Tenant Restrictions.2) User from tenant B, accesses an application from tenant B, from a different network than tenant A's network ( basically external to tenant A's network )In this scenario, a sign-in log will be generated with all the details in                 -- Sign in logs of Tenant B3) User from tenant B, accesses an application from tenant B, from tenant A's network where the proxy devices have been configured to insert headers in the traffic to Azure AD, as per the tenant restrictions feature mentioned above.In this scenario, the sign in logs will be as follows :                 -- Sign in log containing all information in tenant B's sign ins , as usual.                 -- Sign in log containing partial information in tenant A's Sign in logs.                 -- Sign in log containing partial information in tenant A's Azure Active Directory blade > Overview > Other Capabilities > Tenant Restrictions.The partial information will show up in the format :User: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000Username: domainName.comUser ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000Alternate sign-in name: Application: ApplicationXYZApplication ID: Resource: ResourceNameXYZResource ID:  Client app:  where domainName.com is the domain of the user who is trying to login. ( In our scenario, this will be TenantB's domain name )
solution: The customer will have to take this Tenant Restrictions' sign in logs into considerations when they run scripts to analyze the logs.

symptom: Users/Customers claim that they cannot see their Line-Of-Business applications on Azure AD's Conditional Access Application picker, when creating new Conditional Access policies.
rootCause: This can happen when the user has created the application as a native app, or does not have a Web App platform configured under its Platform Configurations in Authentication sections of the application's app registration portal.Conditional Access does not apply to native apps , because it wouldn't provide any real security. A caller can still just always call the API directly.Hence, the user needs to protect the APIs that the native application is trying to access, with Conditional Access, rather than protecting the Native Application itself.For info on what is a native application : https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/native-app
solution: The resolution on this is to apply Conditional Access to the available APIs that the application is invoking/accessing, or to the service that the API falls under.

symptom: While joining a Linux VM in a step we need to run kinit <User's UPN> this step may throw the below errors,kinit: Cannot find KDC for realm '<Domain FQDN>' while getting initial credentialskinit: Resource temporarily unavailable while getting initial credentialsIn this article we will see why the error occurs and how to troubleshoot and resolve them.What is kinit?kinit is a command in Linux operating system which is used to obtain and cache an initial TGT(Ticket Granting Ticket) from the DC.
rootCause: kinit: Cannot find KDC for realm '<Domain FQDN>' while getting initial credentialsNo definition for the domain FQDN in krb5.conf file.kinit: Resource temporarily unavailable while getting initial credentialsThe nameserver records are missing in resolv.conf file.
solution:
1) kinit: Cannot find KDC for realm '<Domain FQDN>' while getting initial credentials
 This
     error occurs if there is no definition in the /etc/krb5.conf file for the
     kdc of domain you are trying to join the machine to.(Usually the domain is
     picked from the UPN suffix).
 If
     this error occurs, please follow the below steps to add the entry in krb5
     file.
 Run
     the command vi /etc/krb5.conf press "i" to start write mode in
     vim editor.
 Add
     the entry in the below syntax after the [realms] tag in the file,<Domain
FQDN in capital letters> = {kdc =
<dc fqdn or IP Address>kdc =
<dc2 fqdn or IP Address>}
 Save
     and close the file by pressing escape key and wq followed by an enter.
 Now
     try running kinit.2) kinit: Resource temporarily unavailable while getting initial credentials1.     
This error occurs
because the nameserver record is missing in the /etc/resolv.conf file2.     
Open the
resolv.conf file using the command vi /etc/resolv.conf3.     
Add the entry as
below,nameserver
<IP of DC>nameserver
<IP of DC2>4.     
Save and close the file by pressing escape key
and wq followed by an enter.5.     
Now try running kinit.Note:If user's UPN suffix and the created domain name is different use <user samAccountname>@<Domain FQDN> this will join the machine to domain because Linux actually thinks the UPN suffix of the user is the domain and searches for the UPN suffix as the Domain. For example, If the user domain is "a.local" and username is "human@a.com" while joining to the domain use "human@a.local" this will search for the user "human" in "a.local" so, this will succeed and the machine will join to the domain.

This article describes how to unjoin a Windows 10 computer that has been Microsoft Entra joined to a Microsoft Entra tenant.  OOBE Setup in Windows 10 Enterprise presents the user with two "domain join" options to choose from.  
   Join Azure AD   Choose this option if your organization uses O365 or other business services from Microsoft. Your organization might collect information about you, install or remove apps, change settings or disable features, delete content, or reset your device. Talk to your support staff to learn more.  
  
   
  
  Join a domain   If you plan to join your PC to a domain, we'll help you set up a local account now. When setup is finished, join the domain as you have in the past. 
  
   
   The first option allows you to join the device to Microsoft Entra.  This allows a user to enter a UPN logon name in the form of JoeUser@contoso.com. If Device Registration is enabled on the Microsoft Entra tenant, then the device can join.  Microsoft Entra join injects the user's SID from Microsoft Entra ID into the local Administrators group of the computer. Currently, Microsoft Entra join does not create a computer object in the on-premises Active Directory.  The second option is to legacy domain join to an Active Directory domain. Choosing the second option has you create a local account on the computer first. Once you are logged on with the local administrator account you can join the computer to a domain.  The user selected the first option during OOBE and performed a Microsoft Entra join because the Microsoft Entra tenant had Device Registration enabled.  The user logged on successfully but discovered they cannot access all of their domain resources. rootCause: "Source Attribute" field would not let you type anything static. The alphabets will keep on deleting as you type them.

symptom: Enable the debug logs by following this article https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureAD/_wiki/wikis/AzureAD/183983/Azure-AD-Sign-in-for-Azure-Linux-VMs?anchor=auth.log-(debug-level)in the log you will see the below error,vmTextMining sshd[48164]: pam_aad(sshd:auth): Cannot find the cloud instance for location CentralIndiaThis issue is caused because the metadata sends the location name as IndiaCentral and Plugin is searching for CentralIndia.
rootCause: This issue is caused because the metadata sends the location name as IndiaCentral and Plugin is searching for CentralIndia.
solution: This issue is fixed in the Plugin release 1.0.011360001 to install this plugin please follow the below steps,Install the extension with the below command,az vm extension set \--publisher Microsoft.Azure.ActiveDirectory.LinuxSSH \--name AADLoginForLinux \--resource-group <rg name> \--vm-name <vm name>Login to the VM with a local root account.Create a directory under home directory named plugin using the command mkdir plugin.Navigate to plugin directory using cd plugin.Run the command for Ubuntu version 18.04 curl -O https://packages.microsoft.com/ubuntu/18.04/prod/pool/main/a/aadlogin/aadlogin_1.0.011360001_amd64.deb for Ubuntu version 16.04 use curl -O https://packages.microsoft.com/ubuntu/16.04/prod/pool/main/a/aadlogin/aadlogin_1.0.011360001_amd64.debRun the command apt remove aadlogin.Once the previous command completes run the command dpkg -i aadlogin_1.0.011360001_amd64.debNow if you run the command apt show aadlogin the version will be 1.0.011360001This should update the Plugin set to the recent version and if the install is successful you will get a response as shown in the screenshot below,Restart the sshd service.Now when you try login the devicelogin flow will be triggered.

Taking Examples : App name : <Application Name> with App ID : 2fddb45a-3701-403d-8063-69c4f8531838Attribute value ( Role ) : Service-based_Development_OperationsAttribute name in analysis : displayNameUnique Identifier for the role: idCustomer's Application that is setup for provisioning, when it goes to quarantine, gives the following logs (or you figure this out using the Kusto Queries) :Here is the current reason for the quarantine: EncounteredQuarantineException.The code for the recent error code was DuplicateAppRoles.Here is the most recent error message: "This Azure Active Directory service principal has app roles with duplicate attribute values: 2fddb45a-3701-403d-8063-69c4f8531838: "<Application Name>".  The attribute with the duplicate values is displayName.  The duplicated value is Add:"Service-based_Development_Operations" (Source).  The synchronization job cannot proceed until the duplication is remedied.  One way of remedying the duplication would be to edit the service principal using the Azure Active Directory Graph or the Microsoft Graph.  Both of those Web application programming interfaces are documented on the World Wide Web.  If the documentation is insufficient, please file a request for support using the Microsoft Azure Active Directory Graph or the Microsoft Graph via Azure support.
rootCause: Usually when this happens, the cause is that the role that currently exists in the application with that name is not the same role that previously had it. Each role has a unique identifier -  "id" in this case. During the initial times of the application�s creation and usage, AAD would have imported the available roles into the application object for usage, out of which, "Service-based_Development_Operations" is one of them Our AAD service sees the current role in <Application Name> and sees that no role exists in AAD with the ID value of 67890 ( example number used for the role name Service_Based_Development_Operations ) . It then tries to create the role, but encounters a duplicate attribute conflict as there is an existing role with the name Service-based_Development_Operations already (which was imported initially), but it has a different anchor/ID attribute value 12345 ( Example number again that is essentially different from the current value of ID for the same role in <Application Name> ) . At the current moment, our service does not resolve such mismatches by itself.Hence the new incoming role is conflicting with the role that we currently have for this application in Azure AD ( Conflict with display name being same for existing role with a given ID, and an incoming role with a new ID )The solution here would be to delete the role for the serviceprincipal object through Graph. Once that is done, the incoming role should not have any problems in getting created on our side and hence, would have you avoid the current situation of duplicate Roles . ( You will need to use a PATCH command to update the approle for the service principal through graph, where the body will contain the updated app roles list. To get the current list for the service principal you can run a GET command on graph as well for the service principal object)PS: Huge thanks to Danny Zollner who helped me understand the cause!
solution: 1) First, take a look in ASC using the AppId in the right tenant, to see if this role ( you can search with the id  ) is present as part of the serviceprincipal's roles, or the application's roles definition. ASC > Azure AD Explorer > Applications > AppId > Service Principal / ApplicationObject2) Making a note of the object ID of the service principal / application object where you noticed the AppRole under investigation was presentRun the following in graph Explorer ( Sign in first there ) :GET https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/serviceprincipals/02c5872b-a48e-4892-802d-86ecda0fed5e Format : https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/serviceprincipals/ObjectIDofServicePrincipal  Get the approles definition from here.For instance : "appRoles": [        {            "allowedMemberTypes": [                "User"            ],            "description": "User",            "displayName": "User",            "id": "4fe59fae-f463-4713-857a-ae9e82a35498",            "isEnabled": true,            "origin": "Application",            "value": null        },        {            "allowedMemberTypes": [                "User"            ],            "description": "msiam_access",            "displayName": "msiam_access",            "id": "0931ab05-cb33-4fb9-a3ea-c56f9d06f312",            "isEnabled": true,            "origin": "Application",            "value": null        },        {            "allowedMemberTypes": [                "User"            ],            "description": "Test for user/group",            "displayName": "User/group role",            "id": "4fe59fae-f463-4713-857a-ae9e82a35628",            "isEnabled": true,            "origin": "Application",            "value": "grouptest"        }    ] -- The highlighted role for instance is the one that was being complained about, and is the one we need to remove, so that we can import the new role --3) Edit this to first disable the role in question. PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/serviceprincipals/02c5872b-a48e-4892-802d-86ecda0fed5eformat : PATCH sameURL body:  (note the additional { and } to make this a json formatted body )New app role definition like below :{    "appRoles": [        {            "allowedMemberTypes": [                "User"            ],            "description": "User",            "displayName": "User",            "id": "4fe59fae-f463-4713-857a-ae9e82a35498",            "isEnabled": true,            "origin": "Application",            "value": null        },        {            "allowedMemberTypes": [                "User"            ],            "description": "msiam_access",            "displayName": "msiam_access",            "id": "0931ab05-cb33-4fb9-a3ea-c56f9d06f312",            "isEnabled": true,            "origin": "Application",            "value": null        },        {            "allowedMemberTypes": [                "User"            ],            "description": "Test for user/group",            "displayName": "User/group role",            "id": "4fe59fae-f463-4713-857a-ae9e82a35628",            "isEnabled": false,            "origin": "Application",            "value": "grouptest"        }    ] } 4) Now, to remove the role mentioned, keeping the formatting in mind when you do that. ( Commas, and the paranthesis ) [ In the example above, delete the entire highlighted part, along with the comma above that part to have the new app role definition ready.Once that is done, change the mode to PatchPATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/serviceprincipals/02c5872b-a48e-4892-802d-86ecda0fed5eformat : PATCH sameURL body:New app role definition like below :{    "appRoles": [        {            "allowedMemberTypes": [                "User"            ],            "description": "User",            "displayName": "User",            "id": "4fe59fae-f463-4713-857a-ae9e82a35498",            "isEnabled": true,            "origin": "Application",            "value": null        },        {            "allowedMemberTypes": [                "User"            ],            "description": "msiam_access",            "displayName": "msiam_access",            "id": "0931ab05-cb33-4fb9-a3ea-c56f9d06f312",            "isEnabled": true,            "origin": "Application",            "value": null        }        ] }This should remove the app role from the Service principal's definition.Post this, the provisioning should succeed when you restart.

Seamless SSO doesn't work as expected. Users get prompted for Password during sign in.
rootCause: After we hit login.microsoftonline.com, Azure AD detects that the tenant is enabled for seamless SSO and redirects the user as below. User gets Unauthorized challenged from Autologon Endpoint. symptom: We have created ADFS claim rule to block all external request except the particular Active directory group of users. After creating the rule, we still see connections are allowed and the requirement was not satisfied. ADFS is not issuing Deny claims when users in AD group "S-1-5-21-1010517290-1075059173-903097961-179968" comes from external network. Below is the rule we created:exists([Type == "http://schemas.microsoft.com/2012/01/requestcontext/claims/x-ms-proxy"])&& exists([Type == "http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/claims/groupsid", Value =~ "S-1-5-21-1010517290-1075059173-903097961-179968"])&& NOT exists([Type == "http://schemas.microsoft.com/2012/01/requestcontext/claims/x-ms-forwarded-client-ip", Value =~ "\b1\.1\.1\.([1-9]|[1-9][0-9]|1[0-9][0-9]|2[0-5][0-9])\b"])=> issue(Type = "http://schemas.microsoft.com/authorization/claims/deny", Value = "true");The claim rule here is set to issue deny if the user�s authentication comes through ADFS Proxy (WAP) and if the user belong to Group with SID - S-1-5-21-1010517290-1075059173-903097961-179968 and also if the user�s authentication has not originated from the x-ms-forwarded-client-ip location mentioned (which is a trusted IP address)We confirmed the user is a part of the group in Active directory.
rootCause: In ADFS Audit / debug logs, when claim processing was happening, the group was not being evaluated. The group SID S-1-5-21-1010517290-1075059173-903097961-179968 assigned in the ADFS claim rule was for a distribution group and not a Security group.
solution: Changed the group type to Security group and ADFS started processing the group claims and issued deny claim as it should.

Hybrid Azure AD joined machines escrow bitlocker recovery information in Azure AD or not.

Azure AD joined machines take approximately 3 hours  (=2.77 hours) to boot up the logon screen. The machine is not hung and you will see a black screen with spinning dots. 2.77 hours later, after the boot is complete, interactive logon works correctly.The problem happens when the following conditions are met.1. AzureAD is synced on-premises AD.2. Client's Work Group name and Domain NetBIOS name of the on-premises are same.
rootCause: When you logon with AzureAD account synced with on-premises AD, the domain information of the on-premises AD is set to the client OS. If the on-premises Domain NetBIOS name and the Work Group name of the client are the same, the deadlock occurs after the rebooting client.
solution: Change the Work Group name of the client to a different name than the Domain NetBIOS name of the on-premises AD. This bug will be fixed in the future release.

Parameter name: certificate     at System.IdentityModel.Tokens.X509SecurityToken..ctor(X509Certificate2 certificate, String id, Boolean clone, Boolean disposable)     at Microsoft.IdentityServer.Service.Configuration.MSISSecurityTokenServiceConfiguration.Create(Boolean forSaml, Boolean forPassive)     at Microsoft.IdentityServer.Service.Policy.PolicyServer.Service.ProxyPolicyServiceHost.ConfigureWIF()     at Microsoft.IdentityServer.Service.SecurityTokenService.MSISConfigurableServiceHost.Configure()     at Microsoft.IdentityServer.Service.Policy.PolicyServer.Service.ProxyPolicyServiceHost.Create()     at Microsoft.IdentityServer.ServiceHost.STSService.StartProxyPolicyStoreService(ServiceHostManager serviceHostManager)     at Microsoft.IdentityServer.ServiceHost.STSService.OnStartInternal(Boolean requestAdditionalTime)   User Action:   Ensure that the certificate is valid and has not been revoked or expired. ======================    Trying to update the ADFS certificates fails with this error:  Update-AdfsCertificate : Could not connect to net.tcp://localhost:1500/policy. rootCause: This can occur if the ADFS Token Signing and Token Decrypting certificates have expired, and the system was not able to roll over to new certificates. This situation may be most common when using a VM template, or if a VM was shut down during the certificate expiration timeframe.
solution: The ADFS service won't start because the certificates are expired, and you can't update the certificates unless the ADFS service is started.  You have to trick the system into thinking the certificates are valid long enough to get the service started, then you can update the certs.  We can do that with PowerShell:  # Set the system date back to before the certificates expired, and then start the service.   # Run these together so that service starts before the system time automatically changes back. set-date -date "10/01/2022 06:11" start-service adfssrv  # Update the two expired certificates Update-AdfsCertificate -CertificateType "Token-Signing" -Urgent Update-AdfsCertificate -CertificateType "Token-Decrypting" -Urgent

You are using AAD Joined Windows Scenario the
appliance of Manage account protection settings
with endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune | Microsoft Learn. In this policy you have configured to remove(update) the default
Memberships of "Authenticated Users" and "Interactive"
users with the SID values of these categories. In the same policy you have
chosen to add(Update) a AAD group. The issue experienced is that although the
values are being delivered and applied, the evaluation of the AAD group membership
is not working
rootCause:
AAD Group membership evaluation currently does not
work for the built-in group of "Users"
solution:
Two proposed workarounds.  1st Solution    create a new local group add the SID of the AAD
     Group to this newly created local group on the device grant "Allow log on locally" to the local group in order for the members of the group to
     have the necessary permissions to log on to the device remove the local
     "Users" group in order for other users part of the same tenant
     to not have access to the device   The
steps presented above are the manual steps that you can use in order to arrive
to the desired scenario that you have presented to us.   Since
some of the steps presented in the workaround are not available for
configuration, this solution presented will not be completely configurable
through the Intune Service.    2nd Solution (as this solution can be applied remotely, it has been chosen as the main
alternative)      As
such a second workaround that will provide you with a working environment would
be to create a "Settings Catalog policy for User Rights" where you define the
"Administrators" group and all the individual cloud users that you
require access to the device. This workaround will allow you to define the
users that have log on access to the device, and the users list can be modified
from the Intune Portal. All other default members of the "Allow log on
locally" are going to disappear as they are not going to be configured in
the policy, and as such the permissions will be restricted only to
administrators and the users you have configured individually. In a way
the policy itself will act as the group of users that will have access to the
device. Example: AzureAD\example@contoso.com

symptom: Abstract  Customers are reporting the following symptoms:  Excessive AAD auth requests  The throttling of AAD Auth request  Sign-in failure logs contain error 500011 referencing "Microsoft Application Command Service" with App ID 6f7e0f60-9401-4f5b-98e2-cf15bd5fd5e3 as the application with an empty resource on Azure AD Sign-in log with the following text:"errorCode": 500011,"failureReason": "The resource principal named {name} was not found in the tenant named {tenant}. This can happen if the application has not been installed by the administrator of the tenant or consented to by any user in the tenant. You might have sent your authentication request to the wrong tenant.","additionalDetails": "Developer error - the app requested access to a resource (application) that isn't installed in your tenant. If you expect the app to be installed, you may need to provide administrator permissions to add it. Check with the developers of the resource and application to understand what the right setup for your tenant is."   The issue occurs following ORGID logons to AAD-joined Windows 10 and Windows 11 devices that have installed the Windows Backup application contained in Windows Updates released on and after:  Windows 10 22H2  Windows 11 22H2   KB 5029331: August 22, 2023�KB5029331 (OS Build 19045.3393) Preview KB 5030211: September 12, 2023�KB5030211 (OS Builds 19044.3448 and 19045.3448)     KB 5031354: October 10, 2023�KB5031354 (OS Build 22621.2428) KB 5031455: October 31, 2023�KB5031455 (OS Builds 22621.2506 and 22631.2506) Preview    The issue occurs due a complex interaction between Windows backup and AFC when an ORGID account logs onto an AAD-domain joined device that installed a Windows Update containing Windows Backup. The role of AFC is to perform the bidirectional, background data transfer for user, app and device state between on-prem and cloud (i.e. user state roaming). Specifically, error 2147746053, 0x80040105, E_CDP_NOT_FOUND occurs during the attempt to back up icons for AAD accounts. AFC contacts AAD TokenBroker to determine if a user ticket exists for the https://assets.activity.microsoft.com endpoint. Upon finding none, AFC returns a non-transient "not found" error code which is repeated every time AFC attempts a backup to AAD after an ORGID User has logged onto Window Backup enabled, AAD-joined Windows client.    Status Updates November 29, 2023: Fixes shipping in 2023 11D on November 30, 2023 prevent new auth requests (that would fail to authenticate) from accumulating in the AFC's queue. Additional fixes tentatively scheduled for release fix in 2024 1D++ will cleanup the already-accumulated-and-forever-failing requests from the AFC's queue. Point: Pre-10B-patched devices could lead with the 2023 11D Update and avoid queue growth.  November 28, 2023: PG reports 1-month best case to develop, test and ship a tool resulting in release date close to the official fix currently targeting 2024 1D. Point: PG favors a released fix. and NOT shipping an executable.  November 6, 2023. Minor edits made to improve readability. The players have gone radio silent and there are no new insights on release dates, fix scope et al. # of impacted tenants identified. KB created based on insights from Anthony Fontaino <Anthony.Fontaino@microsoft.com>; Irina Salvan <irinai@microsoft.com>; Derek Menzies <derekmenzies@microsoft.com>     Resources ICM 427009738: [Identity] Generic Cloud Identity CRI - Sign-in error code 500011 with empty Resource ID Filed on behalf of case 2309190030002533  ICM 435331109: [Mercury] Backup app suddenly appears in Recent and recommended applications on Enterprise devices but cannot be run sparking IT Pro dissatisfaction ICM filed on behalf of the auto-install of Windows Backup on enterprise / domain joined SKUs that can't actually run Windows Backup.   Internal KB5031666: Servicing: 9B.23: Windows Backup Application auto installed on Windows 10 21H2 \ 22H2 devices by 2023 9B WU KB5029331 KB documenting the installation of Windows Backup.  Bugs listed in the "Resolution" and "More Information" section of this article   Is context:  This issue is exposed by installing Windows Updates that contain Windows Backup on Windows 10 21H2 / 22H2 and Windows 11 22H2 devices released on and after  September 12 that install Windows Backup on Windows 10 22H2devices (all SKUs) October 10 that install Windows Backup on Windows 11 22H2 devices (all SKUs)  Symptom: Logging on with an ORG ID account to a Windows backup-enabled, AAD-joined Windows 10 or Windows 11 computer generate unwanted and excessive AAD Auth, AAD Auth throttling and error 500011 sign-in failures.  AFC maintains a DB + supporting cache containing entries that generate AAD authentication requests. Entries are added by a background task in AFC with a smaller number added by scheduled tasks by Window Backup. The # of entries in this cache continually increase with system uptime by an ORGID user account to an AAD-joined, WB-enabled device, increasing the # of AAD auth requests and 50011 errors by problem in-state devices.  Locking the logon session of an ORGID account does NOT halt AAD logon request on problem in-state devices.  Clearing the AFC cache containing entries that generate AAD auth requests requires clearing a table in a DB or deleting the entire DB Ask: What about when the device is hibernated? Do auth requests wake up the device? Current least bad workaround is to iteratively delete the AFC DB which requires turning off the AFC service but the current service owner has never done this, especially at enterprise scale.    IS NOT context  Legacy domain joined devices and workgroup computers are NOT impacted by this issue.  ORGID Logons in an application on a domain-joined or workgroup computer are NOT impacted by this issue.   Auth requests DO NOT occur on a device where the ORGID accounts has logged off from an AAD-joined device OR when the device is shut down.    Workarounds and Solutions Disable settings sync (either in the UI or through Group Policy) Open Services in Windows and stop the CDPUserSvc_* service. Delete the folders under %LocalAppData%\ConnectedDevicesPlatform.The DB is located under %localappdata%\ConnectedDevicesPlatform\<some directory based on user id>\ActivitiesCache* Reboot. ORGID users should logoff from Windows Backup-enabled, AAD-joined devices Install the 11D Windows Update when it becomes available. The installation of this fix in combination with deleting the AFC cache will halt unwanted AAD auth.  Monitor this KB for status updates.   Non-Workarounds Each Windows Update contains protections for between 70-90 CVEs. All Updates are cumulative. Delaying the installation of the 9B+ and 10B and newer Windows Updates exposes devices to known, mitigated bugs and security vulnerabilities.  AAD auth overhead triggered by Windows Backup scheduled tasks is minimal. Do not delete or pause Windows Backup Scheduled tasks in an effort to reduce unwanted AAD auth requests.  Removing 9B or 10B and superseding Windows Updates does NOT clear entries in the AFC cache that trigger unwanted AAD auth requests while such updates were installed.  Windows Backup is system-level application that cannot be removed except by uninstalling the corresponding Windows Updates. Unsupported attempts to remove Windows Backup may cause servicing stack corruption that prevents the installation of future Windows Updates.  Adding the "Microsoft Application Command Service" (App ID 6f7e0f60-9401-4f5b-98e2-cf15bd5fd5e3 audience to the tenant breaks zero exhaust goal.  Filing GETHELPS and executive escalations will not accelerate the release of solutions.  Blocking access to the https://assets.activity.microsoft.com endpoint has NO impact. This issue is NOT a problem with connecting to the web service endpoint but in acquiring the authentication token for it.  (i.e. the issue occurs before the client even attempts to contact the endpoint.) There is no toggle for the Apps backup control hence, there is no toggle that turns off the MicrosoftDevice.Apps policy. The apps toggle status is statically defined for non-MSA user accounts.  Changing the value for "Share across devices" in the "Shared Experiences" section of the Windows Settings applet as NO / ZERO / NADA impact on this issue. Screenshot  Text version       Shared ExperiencesLet apps on other devices including linked phones and tablets open and message apps on this device and vice versa  On | Off    On Win10....the Settings page that involves sync state resides in "Sync your settings" under Accounts.  Turning it off would prevent new stuff from going into the queue, but it doesn't affect existing items already in the queue.   Symptoms  Azure tenants are reporting sign-in failure logs with error 500011 referencing "Microsoft Application Command Service" as the application with an empty resource on Azure AD Sign-in log. ASC may indicate an auth failure every 3 seconds. The ErrorCode: 50011 field contains the following text: "errorCode": 500011 and "failureReason" txt for AppDisplayName": "Microsoft Application Command Service"      Sign-in error code: 500011,Failure Reason"The resource principal named {name} was not found in the tenant named {tenant}. This can happen if the application has not been installed by the administrator of the tenant or consented to by any user in the tenant. You might have sent your authentication request to the wrong tenant.", Additional Details":"Developer error - the app requested access to a resource (application) that isn't installed in your tenant. If you expect the app to be installed, you may need to provide administrator permissions to add it. Check with the developers of the resource and application to understand what the right setup for your tenant is."   The caller App "Microsoft Application Command Service" (App ID 6f7e0f60-9401-4f5b-98e2-cf15bd5fd5e3) is making a call to obtain a token to the Resource:  "https://assets.activity.microsoft.com" However, such resource does not exist on the resource Tenant (ID<GUID>), hence the expected exception:  AADSTS500011: The resource principal named https://assets.activity.microsoft.com was not found in the tenant named <Tenant name> Group. This can happen if the application has not been installed by the administrator of the tenant or consented to by any user in the tenant. You might have sent your authentication request to the wrong tenant.    To understand the exact action that is being performed and why, the best option is to engage the caller app owners:  https://microsoftservicetree.com/services/314d9ae1-34e6-4ec0-bb42-36a834ce893a#    <ph>  Issue History (Microsoft Internal and NOT to be shared outside of Microsoft for any reason including with NDA partners, customers or OEMs)    PDR has a series of sync policies that control whether data is synced or not between on an-prem client and the cloud.  There are three sets of these policies  original,  backup,  11x11    There is only one set of UI controls.  In July 2023, policies were found to be out of sync in such a way that backup was effectively permanently disabled, so code was written to force sync them but unfortunately, overrode turning off the sync data for backup items when the user was AAD. Exacerbating this change, the app backup code used this policy setting to determine whether it needed to run or not, so it found Windows Backup enabled and ran.  The normal codepath in CDS (cloud data store) suppressed upload of normal data items when the caller is an AAD user, but when the backup entities were initially added, a new CDN-like API was added for apps backup to store app icons.  This API had no suppression. The AFC (our transport layer) API that handled the actual send to the server requires token in the background and requires 2FA On the server side, it was never expected that AAD would be needed for app backup, so it rejects all calls with AAD tokens  When AFC gets a token rejection, it invalidates the local token broker cache, which apparently causes the token broker to report AAD errors when it next attempts to fetch a token to send to our server. WSD has a fix that prevent new items from being added to the AFC cache, currently targeting 11D.  Current efforts are focused on clearing existing items stuck in the AFC cache. The July 2023 change is protected by a velocity switch, but that only solves the same "getting into AFC" problem targeted by the 11D fix) and has a limited lifecycle.  A second fix is required to purge existing entries in the AFC cache is scheduled for late January 2024.   Impact of deleting CDS CDS = cloud data store, a cache of data returned to callersData is roaming vs. backup. Roaming data comes from cloud. This data is NOT lost when deleting the CDS unless changes made locally have not been uploaded to the server  Backup data is at some risk to getting lost but most backup state is maintained locally.    Impact of Settings App UI for Backup Pane vs. GP  GP settings defines Admin intent. Settings UI defines user intent  Setting either value to "no" or disabled disables Sync  Any disabled value wins over enabled regardless of its origin (i.e. GP enabled does not trump configuring the Setting UI to disabled.  The two GP settings areDisableSettingSyncUserOverride DisableApplicationSettingSyncUserOverride  Code reviews have since revealed that neither setting has impact on entries getting cached by AFC.
